customer_anxiety_profile: Mike arrived at this call with a pre-loaded prior-promise belief ('you guys tell me nothing out of pocket') and framed the call as a bait-and-switch before Brandon said anything substantive. His hostility softened to tentative openness only after Brandon confirmed the promise and offered a no-obligation framing. He is not enthusiastic — he agreed to 'take a look,' which is a low-commitment posture. The closer should expect a skeptical, show-me customer who will hold the 'nothing out of pocket' promise as a baseline expectation.
unresolved_concerns: Mike's prior-promise belief ('nothing out of pocket, never') was confirmed unconditionally — the qualifier ('as long as you qualify') was added but not emphasized. The closer must establish what 'qualifying' means before the proposal lands.
No verify data was collected — the closer has no bill amount, no roof age, no title/co-owner information, and no stated interest anchor to work from.
The appointment was framed as 'no obligation, just take a look' — the closer should expect low commitment and may need to re-anchor the purpose of the meeting at the top of the call.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior promise from Ion marketing/form copy: Mike believes Ion promised him free solar with no out-of-pocket cost. The promise came from marketing materials, not a human rep (Mike confirmed 'No' when asked if he spoke with someone from Ion).
Implicit bait-and-switch concern: Mike framed the call as Ion promising one thing and then asking about his finances — the closer should address this directly and early.
exact_promises_rep_made: 'Never. You will never have to pay out of your own pocket.' (00:55) — unconditional confirmation of the prior-promise belief.
'As long as you qualify for the program' (01:02) — qualifier added but not emphasized; likely did not land as a condition.
'There's no obligation.' (01:48) — low-commitment framing for the appointment.
closer_should_know: Mike's prior-promise belief ('nothing out of pocket, never') came from Ion's marketing/form copy — not from a human rep. This is a marketing-expectation gap, not a rep-promise violation, which makes the repair conversation softer: 'What you saw in our materials is accurate — the program is designed so qualified homeowners don't pay out of pocket. Let me show you exactly how that works for your home.' Lead with this reframe before the proposal. Mike agreed to 'take a look' with no obligation framing — re-anchor the purpose of the meeting at the top of the call before moving into the design.
